sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds: Battle Born Pond, Davis Creek Pond, Idlewild Ponds, Marilyn's Pond, Paradise Ponds, Rancho San Rafael Pond, Sparks Marina Pond, Verdi Pond, Virginia Lake and Wilson Common. Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours parks are open to public use.

sources.title: Davis Creek Pond — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Davis Creek Park Pond is a natural shallow lake that has been enhanced over the years to its current size of 2.5 acres and a depth of 15 feet. Fed by Davis Creek that flows intermittently and a ditch that brings water from Ophir Creek, Davis Creek Park Pond usually remains full. The pond is managed as a put-and-take fishery and trout are generally caught shortly after being planted.

sources.title: Davis Creek Park Pond Angler and Fish Survey Information — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Nevada Department of Wildlife first stocked Largemouth Bass in the early 1980's and trout have been stocked since 1991. Open year around, during public park hours. Limit is 3 game fish.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Davis Creek Pond is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit is stated for Davis Creek Pond in NDOW's current regulation table; the limit applies to the combined daily/possession total across all game fish species.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds (incl. Davis Creek Pond)
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (all species combined) at Davis Creek Pond.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish.

parameters.note: NDOW's commission-regulation table lists hours for Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds generically as 'hours parks are open to public use'; NDOW's own Davis Creek Park Pond angler survey confirms 'during public park hours' with no fixed clock times.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing at Davis Creek Pond is allowed during the hours the park is open to the public.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours parks are open to public use

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older; no water-specific permit exists for Davis Creek Pond.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older at Davis Creek Pond; no license is required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
